---
applyTo: '**/*.ts,**/*.tsx'
description: Rules for user-facing strings and locale-specific values.
---

# Internationalization

## Define Messages, Don't Hardcode

All user-facing text and locale-specific values (separators, date formats) must use `defineMessages` from the project's i18n library. English is the default `defaultMessage`. Always include a `description` to give translators context.

```ts
const messages = defineMessages({
  emailLabel: {
    defaultMessage: 'Email',
    description: 'Label for the email input on the sign-in form.',
  },
  decimalSeparator: {
    defaultMessage: '.',
    description: 'Decimal separator (e.g. "." for 1.23 or "," for 1,23).',
  },
});
```

Do **not** use `defineMessages` for internal constants, technical identifiers, or code comments.

Never accept translated strings as props. Components source all text via `useI18n()` internally.

## Workflow

After adding, modifying, or removing any `defineMessages` entry, run the repo-wide extraction command before considering the task complete (e.g. `pnpm run -w i18n` or the equivalent for your repo). Missing this step ships outdated translation catalogs.
